platformIntro: Adobesphere is a student-built platform that brings Adobe's creative world into one organized, easy-to-explore space. Instead of jumping across multiple pages and announcements, you can discover upcoming events, fresh blog stories, and standout creators in one continuous experience.

The project is designed for learners, professionals, and enthusiasts who want to stay connected to Adobe's evolving creative community. From workshops and conference highlights to practical product insights, the platform helps people find the content that fits their interests and turn inspiration into action.

At its core, this platform celebrates the people behind the work: designers, developers, marketers, photographers, and creators shaping the future of digital creativity. While it is not an official Adobe property, it is built with admiration for the ecosystem and a goal of making community knowledge more accessible to everyone.

madeBy.name: Sudeepa Santhanam
madeBy.bio: I'm Sudeepa Santhanam, an MCA student interested in building practical, real-world tech solutions. I primarily work on frontend and full-stack projects using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Tailwind CSS, and I'm comfortable turning ideas into functional web applications. I have experience working with databases like MySQL and PostgreSQL, and I've built projects that integrate frontend, backend, and data handling. While I've worked with Python in the past, my current focus is more on UI development and improving my overall development workflow. I enjoy working in team environments, communicating ideas clearly, and learning by building. I've taken up leadership roles during college events, including being recognized as Best IT Manager at national-level fests, and I was also part of a Datathon where I worked on data analysis and visualization.

timeline.milestone: Defined MVP scope and core use-cases (save, register, comment).
Locked pillars: Events, Blogs, and Creator Profiles.
Mapped end-to-end UX flows and finalized page sets.
Designed shared layout (navbar, footer, toast system).
Structured JSON data and curated platform assets.
Scaffolded folder hierarchy and safety defaults (CSP).
Built UI system (tokens, cards, buttons, form styles).
Implemented responsive navigation and scroll reveal effects.
Created utility library for fetching and formatting data.
Built Explore engine with live search and category filters.
Built dynamic Event, Blog, and Creator detail pages.
Stabilized routing with Router module and appPath helpers.
Introduced Storage layer for localStorage persistence.
Built full Auth system (Signup/Login) with state management.
Developed block-based Blog Editor with autosave functionality.
Added flexible image handling and base64 upload support.
Polished accessibility and responsive edge-case handling.
Validated end-to-end journeys and prepared project demos.
Refined Platform Journey with auto-scrolling carousel.
Added hover-pause and smooth scroll animation effects.
Built user dashboards and editable profile features.
Implemented commenting system and content aggregation.
Migrated static content to inline HTML for simplicity.
Simplified core JS modules for better maintainability.
Resolved creator identity bugs and navigation mismatches.
Cleaned storage keys for consistent session management.
Eliminated FOUC to improve Lighthouse performance scores.
Completed final testing and verified all user flows.

faq.question: How do I register for an event?
faq.answer: Go to the Explore page, open any event card, and click Register. You'll need to be signed in first — create a free account to get started.

faq.question: How do I save a blog or event?
faq.answer: Click the bookmark icon on any blog or event card. You'll need to sign in first — your saved items will appear in your profile dashboard.

faq.question: How do I write and publish my own blog?
faq.answer: Open the Blog Editor, create your post using the block editor, add the required metadata, and click Publish.

faq.question: How do I update my profile information?
faq.answer: Visit My Profile while signed in. You can edit your information directly from the profile page.

faq.question: How do I view all creators on the platform?
faq.answer: Visit the Explore page and switch to the Creators tab to browse all creators.

faq.question: Is this platform free to use?
faq.answer: Yes — the Adobesphere platform is completely free to explore, use, and contribute to.
